This list is also a spoiler-free companion list to my (spoiler full) Morimens timeline project, where I ...

come to think of it
why are there no genesis units native to kadath?
the closest we have are the aeons and ||keeper|| but they dont have any "further" counterparts to compare with
cuz we live in kadath
we're the normal ones
wdym? is the nature of kadath such that its impossible for genesis units to happen?
is kadath the "everyones happy ending" world?
yeah
like inherently in order for a genesis to occur, some shit needs to happen to a character in order for their character arc to develop in a way where they establish a link with a higher will, right
but by nature of being the main world with a protagonist, we're the happy ending world
i.e., we save everyone before bad shit can happen to them
so it's somewhat of a meta explanation but basically genesis characters don't really have the opportunity to happen in kadath because we protag power save them first
(is my thinking, at least)
wow imagine being a genesis character, going through all the suffering just to find out that theres a world where everything went right instead
or having hope only to find out even in the best possible outcome your life would still be shit regardless
Murphy vs GMurphy be like
that's why i'm not happy with gmurphy!!!
she's still sad
what is this gem thing i want us to bring the real gmurphy back

while genesis units are supposed to be closer to their source, is that a for certain always true rule? could be that some of our awakeners are the 'genesis' version but we wouldn't know or consider them that
or i guess even
selection bias
we only awaken/tend to encounter the ones who are closer
selection bias was my assumption too but it's kinda weird that if that were the case we havent met any "further" counterparts yet
there's probably a murphy out there with an even worse ending than gmurphy but because they're even further from their source, maybe they don't have the gnosis index to be an awakener
like, not even a shittier caecus so that our og one is actually a Gaecus?
like a murphy that gets declared a heretic and never even gets the fetus
well lorewise genesis characters are technically special in a way that isn't just "oh they're from a parallel universe"
like my initial thought was "well if genesis characters are just parallel universe characters, then obviously there are none because you can't be a parallel universe character in your home universe"
but then i went to doublecheck
caecus is so weak that gcaecus can't even live <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>
and it does say that basically a higher order being pollutes the awakening process in order for them to be genesis
this also says the records are limited, so it's theoretically possible there's a less close one that mythag just hasn't encountered
the eternal problem of morimens lore: no certain information ever <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>

embeds.title: Human cannibalism
embeds.description: Human cannibalism is the act or practice of humans eating the flesh or internal organs of other human beings. A person who practices cannibalism is called a cannibal. The meaning of "cannibalism" has been extended into zoology to describe animals consuming parts of individuals of the same species as

Alistair knows a lot about cooking, but due to his state he can neither cook nor eat, yeah. So he ends up sitting in the corner backseating you the entire the (but in a nice way). Since he seems to be, like, a father figure for the keeper, I think he'd mostly tell them about healthier foods, but every once in a while he'd give them a dessert recipe as a reward and then live vicariously through them as they ate it.
Herbert... hm. I'd say he's actually better at baking than cooking, the exact instructions and measurements help the outcome, but he prefers cooking. He'd come up with creative recipe variations all the time and sometimes it's good but most of the time it's inedible. He never tells you which it is when he's making you eat it. But yes, he does give the vibe of someone who likes a good soup. The only consistently edible thing he makes is gourmet catfood
